_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
This document covers the 2023 IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships (Resolution MEPC.377(80)), adopted at the 80th session of the Marine Environment Protection Committee (MEPC 80), 3–7 July 2023. It synthesises the strategy's targets, the implementing framework under development (Global Fuel Standard and carbon pricing mechanism), and the current procedural status as of the date of this brief.

**Convention note:** Ocean is the tertiary transport mode for the workspace. The IMO GHG Strategy does not directly regulate freight forwarders. Its effects reach the workspace indirectly: through carrier cost structures, fuel surcharge pass-through, contract terms on long-haul ocean legs, and client sustainability disclosure requirements. Every section below is filtered through that indirect-effect lens.

**Coverage boundary:** MEPC 80 also addressed ballast water management, biofouling, marine litter, underwater noise, and ship-to-ship transfers. Those items are outside the scope of this brief. Only GHG Strategy items are analysed here.

**Status of post-MEPC 80 developments (existing brief preview, labeled for navigation only):** The existing brief preview references MEPC 83 (April 2025) framework approval, an MEPC ES.2 adjournment (October 2025), and MEPC 84 formal adoption scheduled for Spring 2026 with entry into force from 2028. These post-MEPC 80 developments are not confirmed by the source content provided for this regeneration. They are carried forward from the prior brief as context but are **not independently sourced in this run**. Each is labeled "unconfirmed in this regeneration" where referenced. Legal and operational decisions should not rest on these unconfirmed dates without independent verification against IMO's current published schedule.

*Source: MEPC 80 Meeting Summary, International Maritime Organization (IMO), 7 July 2023. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/meetingsummaries/pages/mepc-80.aspx*
*Source: Resolution MEPC.377(80) – 2023 IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships, IMO, 7 July 2023. https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/KnowledgeCentre/IndexofIMOResolutions/MEPCDocuments/MEPC.377(80).pdf*
